page: Amazon Web Services m7a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also demonstrate strong capabilities. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes register average speeds. Notably, the server achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model, while medium 7B model inference remains average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory or storage-optimized instances. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the baseline network speed is limited to 6 Gbps. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale AI training. This server is best suited for workloads such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and a GPU, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain average. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, and software compilation, offering a balanced resource profile without the added cost of specialized hardware.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 6 Gbps network baseline, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server with 16 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for reliable single-core and small-model inference workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core CPU and small-model LLM inference performance
No local storage or GPU, with a 6 Gbps baseline network speed
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
